name: Union State Bank
description_short: Union State Bank Winterset, IA — Iowa-chartered community bank established in 1935 with three locations serving individuals, businesses, and farmers acr...
description_long:
Union State Bank is a family-owned, independent Iowa-chartered bank that has been operating since 1935. The bank maintains three physical branch locations in Winterset (main headquarters on HWY 92), West Des Moines, and Truro, positioning itself as a community-focused financial institution rather than a regional or national chain. The bank is FDIC insured and operates as an Equal Opportunity and Housing Lender.

Union State Bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial services across three customer segments: personal banking, business banking, and agricultural banking. Personal services include checking and savings accounts, personal loans, student loans, CDs and IRAs, consumer credit cards, and identity theft protection through ID Theftsmart. Business customers can access business checking, business loans, business credit cards, and merchant services. Agricultural customers receive specialized agricultural checking and loan products. The bank also provides mortgage services including home loans, home equity loans, and real estate financing. Digital banking options include mobile banking, bill pay with Zelle integration, mobile deposit, e-statements, and a 24-hour telebank system accessible at 515-739-3051.

Union State Bank distinguishes itself through its community bank positioning, independent ownership structure, and service to agricultural and rural markets. The bank emphasizes personalized service through "Personal Bankers" and maintains extended branch hours including Saturday service at the Winterset location. The institution provides nationwide ATM access through privileged status ATM partnerships alongside ATMs at all four locations. Products like the "Secure Advantage Checking" account and the "Golden Opportunity Club" demonstrate targeted product development for specific customer needs.

As a small community bank with only three physical locations, Union State Bank's reach and service capabilities are geographically limited compared to regional or national banks. The limited branch footprint may restrict convenience for customers outside the Madison, Dallas, and Polk county areas. While the bank offers digital services and ATM networks, it lacks the extensive technology infrastructure, product breadth, and competitive rates that larger institutions typically provide. For customers seeking only basic banking services in the bank's service area, the community bank model provides personalized service, but those needing specialized products, premium rates, or broader geographic accessibility should compare alternatives. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts including Secure Advantage Checking with identity protection
Personal savings accounts and money market accounts
Home loans, home equity loans, and real estate financing
Mobile banking and bill pay with Zelle integration
pros: FDIC insured with 90+ years of operating history since 1935, demonstrating institutional stability
Three convenient branch locations with extended hours including Saturday service at main Winterset branch
Specialized agricultural banking products and loans designed for farm customers
Nationwide ATM access through privileged status ATM partnerships plus four in-house ATMs
24-hour telebank system (515-739-3051) for account access outside business hours
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile banking, bill pay with Zelle, and mobile deposit
Personal banker model emphasizing relationship-based service rather than transactional banking
Both personal and business credit card options with merchant services for business customers
cons: Only three physical branch locations limit accessibility for customers outside Madison, Dallas, and Polk counties
As a small independent bank, likely offers fewer specialized products and lower competitive rates compared to regional/national banks
Limited information on website about specific APRs, fee structures, and product terms makes rate comparison difficult
Smaller institution may have more limited lending capacity for large business or commercial real estate loans
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or insurance products beyond identity theft protection
best_for: Farmers and agricultural businesses needing specialized ag lending and checking products in central Iowa
Small business owners in Madison, Dallas, and Polk counties seeking personalized relationship banking
Rural and community-focused individuals prioritizing local bank ownership and personal service over rates
Customers in Winterset, West Des Moines, or Truro seeking full-service banking with branch convenience
diagnosis: Union State Bank is best suited for farmers, agricultural businesses, and small business owners in central Iowa (Madison, Dallas, Polk counties) who prioritize community banking relationships and personalized service. The main caveat is that as a small three-location community bank, it has geographic limitations and likely cannot compete on rates or specialized products with larger regional and national institutions, making it primarily appropriate for customers in or near its service territory.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
